title: Motion-aware degradation modeling for remote sensing VSR
focus: Jointly models blur, aliasing, jitter, and compression instead of assuming clean bicubic downsampling.
method: Estimate composite degradation first, then perform temporally aligned restoration.
limitations: The degradation recipe is still synthetic, so real-world transfer remains weak.

title: Temporal alignment under satellite viewpoint drift
focus: Studies temporal alignment failure when camera motion and target motion coexist.
method: Uses drift-robust temporal fusion before upsampling.
limitations: Performance drops on long clips with abrupt scene change.

title: Benchmarking remote sensing VSR evaluation protocols
focus: Compares evaluation setups across satellite and aerial video benchmarks.
method: Standardizes train-test splits, metrics, and downsampling assumptions.
limitations: Benchmark coverage is still narrow for cloud, haze, and low-light conditions.

title: Real-world degradation gaps in aerial video restoration
focus: Analyzes mismatch between synthetic degradations and flight-collected video.
method: Builds a taxonomy of blur, compression, rolling shutter, and atmospheric distortion.
limitations: No unified protocol is provided for cross-paper comparison.

title: Lightweight VSR backbones for edge deployment
focus: Explores efficiency-accuracy tradeoffs for onboard or edge-side restoration.
method: Distills a compact temporal SR backbone with reduced memory pressure.
limitations: Efficiency gains are clear, but recovery of tiny targets is weaker.

title: Open problems in remote sensing video super-resolution
focus: Summarizes unresolved challenges across degradation realism, alignment robustness, and benchmark design.
method: Review-style synthesis of common failure modes.
limitations: The survey highlights problems but does not propose a unified solution.
